summary.angle: Look for direct dependencies that modern Node, Bun, Vite, CSS, or small local helpers can reasonably replace.
Look for dependencies that still work, but may no longer match the repo's preference for platform-native, low-cruft tooling.

target.name: rimraf
target.rationale: Node supports recursive removal through fs.rm/fs.rmSync, and the repo already uses POSIX shell commands in package scripts.

findings.finding: Direct dev dependency in nine manifests.
Only one real script use was found: tempest.games build cleanup.

plan.step: Replace tempest.games build cleanup with rm -rf.

target.name: vite-tsconfig-paths
target.rationale: Vite has native resolve.tsconfigPaths support, so the plugin can likely be replaced in Vite and Vitest configs.

findings.finding: Direct dependency in root, hamr, ingt, rel8, anvl, atom.io, and tempest.games.
Imported by several Vite/Vitest configs.

target.name: colors
target.rationale: Node util.styleText provides stable platform-native terminal styling.

findings.finding: Used only by tsdoc.json in library.ts and verbose.ts.

target.name: cross-env
target.rationale: The repo already relies on shell syntax in scripts, so simple environment assignments may be enough if Windows shell compatibility is not a goal.

findings.finding: Root scripts use it for CI=true.
tempest.games uses it for PREVIEW=true.
atom.io scripts use cross-env without declaring it directly.

target.name: dotenv-and-dotenv-cli
target.rationale: The repo already uses dotenvx at the root, and Bun scripts can load env files directly with --env-file.

findings.finding: tempest.games uses dotenv-cli in two database scripts.
The direct dotenv package did not show source imports in the audit.

target.name: fp-ts-and-io-ts
target.rationale: These look like older ecosystem choices beside the repo's current ArkType/Zod usage. Removal needs a public API check.

findings.finding: rel8 appears to have an unused fp-ts devDependency.
ingt uses small fp-ts helpers in resources.ts.
io-ts is declared by ingt but no source import was found in the audit.

target.name: recoverage
target.rationale: Not obsolete by itself, but it keeps tmp in the lockfile transitively.

findings.finding: Used by coverage scripts in atom.io and treetrunks.
Also declared by venerate.
pnpm-lock.yaml still contains tmp@0.2.5 through this dependency path.

target.name: js-temporal-polyfill
target.rationale: Native Temporal exists in modern Node, but browser availability still needs to match tempest.games' frontend support targets before removing the polyfill.

findings.finding: Used in tempest.games backend and frontend code.

target.name: cron
target.rationale: Could be replaced with a small scheduler for simple jobs, but cron semantics are legitimate when that behavior is desired.

findings.finding: Used by flightdeck update checks.
Used by tempest.games session expiration.

target.name: cachedir
target.rationale: Replaceable with a local helper, but cross-platform cache directory behavior is easy to get subtly wrong.

findings.finding: Used only by varmint.
